title: Legit Logistics
summary: Owner-operated delivery operations platform with quote, tracking, and dispatch workflows.

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
Legit Logistics is an owner-operated delivery operations platform built for a New England logistics business. It connects service marketing, quote requests, dispatch execution, public tracking, and proof-of-delivery workflows in one polished product.

Rather than stopping at a brochure site, the platform supports the real operational journey: customers request quotes, admins create and manage jobs, customers receive trustworthy tracking links, and drivers complete deliveries with proof.

pages.title: Role & Team
pages.content:
I led the product as the Full Stack Developer, shaping both the public-facing experience and the operational core. My work covered the landing experience, quote workflow, admin dashboard, tracking routes, and driver proof interactions.

The interface had to feel personal, reliable, and practical for real dispatch work because the business is owner-operated.

pages.title: Timeline
pages.content: This platform shipped as a phased rollout instead of a single short sprint. Phase one established the brand and quote entry points, phase two built the admin dashboard and delivery creation flow, phase three connected tracking and driver proof workflows, and the final phase focused on responsive polish and production readiness.

pages.title: Industry Context
pages.content:
Small and mid-sized delivery businesses often run on fragmented workflows: calls for quotes, manual confirmations, status updates over text, and proof collected in inconsistent ways. That makes same-day delivery harder to scale and weakens customer trust.

Legit Logistics needed a product that felt premium on the surface while still solving the real operational problem underneath.

pages.title: Friction Points
pages.content: Customers wanted clear pricing conversations, reliable updates, and proof that a delivery was truly in motion. Dispatchers needed one place to create jobs, share tracking, and monitor progress. Drivers needed mobile-friendly status tools instead of heavy desktop workflows.

pages.title: Objectives
pages.content:
The goal was to build a connected quote-to-delivery experience: make service options easy to understand, let customers review and negotiate quotes, give admins a reliable dispatch dashboard, and provide public tracking only when a delivery is genuinely confirmed.

A second objective was to support real-world operational proof through photo capture and signature collection on mobile.

pages.title: Success Criteria
pages.content: Success meant the platform had to work for both trust and speed. Key indicators included under-two-minute job creation in the admin dashboard, clean public tracking, responsive mobile workflows, reliable build quality, and a strong operational story anchored in real delivery metrics.

pages.title: Results
pages.content: The product surfaces strong operational proof points: 15,000+ deliveries completed, a 99.2% on-time rate, 500+ business clients, and 24/7 support coverage. On the admin side, dispatchers can create jobs in under two minutes and move quickly from job setup to shareable tracking.

pages.title: Operational Value
pages.content:
Legit Logistics turns a fragmented delivery workflow into a connected operational system. Quote review, dispatch creation, tracking visibility, and proof-of-delivery now support one another instead of living in separate tools.

That creates a more trustworthy customer experience and a more manageable operations layer for the business.

pages.title: User Research
pages.content: Customer stories and workflow feedback shaped the product. People responded positively to receiving tracking only after a job was truly confirmed, and business customers appreciated having room to discuss price through the quote review and counter-offer flow.

pages.title: Workflow Validation
pages.content: Admin and driver workflows were documented and manually validated as part of the rollout. That work confirmed that the delivery creation flow, tracking-share tools, and field-proof interactions were practical enough for everyday use across desktop and mobile screens.

pages.title: Strategy
pages.content: Verification focused on the workflows that mattered most: quote interactions, admin job creation, public tracking, driver status controls, and responsive behavior. The codebase also includes accessibility and infrastructure tests alongside production build verification.

pages.title: Reliability
pages.content: The documented admin milestone shipped with zero TypeScript errors, a successful production build, and real-time dashboard updates through Supabase. The interface stays usable across desktop and mobile, which is critical for dispatchers and drivers working away from a desk.

pages.title: Customer Experience
pages.content: The customer-facing experience combines a strong services narrative with a trust-building logistics flow. It packages same-day delivery, local and regional coverage, final mile, small moves, and construction logistics into a clear visual system with quote-first calls to action and testimonial support.

pages.title: Admin Dashboard
pages.content: The operational centerpiece is a tabbed admin dashboard where dispatchers can create jobs, inspect details, share links or QR codes, and monitor status. Driver controls then extend the workflow into the field with proof capture and signature collection.

pages.title: Tech Stack
pages.content:
Core: React 18, TypeScript, Vite.

UI: Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, Framer Motion.

Platform: Supabase, TanStack Query, React Hook Form, Zod.

pages.title: Architecture
pages.content: The architecture blends public routes with operational tooling: service pages, quote review routes, order lookup, public tracking, admin authentication, and driver interactions. Supabase powers authentication, data, storage, and subscriptions, while React Query keeps admin state responsive.

pages.title: Roadmap
pages.content: Next priorities include driver assignment, richer notification automation, route optimization, and deeper delivery analytics. The roadmap also includes cleaning up the data model so customer and pricing data live in dedicated tables instead of temporary structures.

pages.title: Key Learnings
pages.content: This project reinforced that logistics UX is really about trust, timing, and operational clarity. Showing tracking at the right moment matters as much as visual polish, and admin-first workflows are often what turns a good-looking site into a genuinely useful product.
